title: rootless: support port redirection from the host
body:
add support for ports redirection from the host.

It needs an updated slirp4netns to work including this PR:

https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns/pull/61

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2081

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body: this is blocked on having an updated slirp4netns
/cc @AkihiroSuda

@giuseppe: GitHub didn't allow me to request PR reviews from the following users: AkihiroSuda.

Note that only [containers members](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) and repo collaborators can review this PR, and authors cannot review their own PRs.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/2142#issuecomment-453561351):

>/cc @AkihiroSuda


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

tests are happy.  We could merge this PR even without the changes in slirp4netns, the only difference is that we won't get the informative error message when port redirection is used.

If used together with slirp4netns from git, then port redirection works with rootless containers
/cc @mheon @baude @rhatdan
@giuseppe Is there any way to detect if slirp4netns supports this, and warn if it doesn't?

> @giuseppe Is there any way to detect if slirp4netns supports this, and warn if it doesn't?

we could parse the `slirp4netns --help` output and see if `--api-socket` is supported
No, lets not attempt to hack it, we should only have the issue for a short time.

> No, lets not attempt to hack it, we should only have the issue for a short time.

I had just submitted a new version that does it :-) Going to revert it
Will it make sense to have `slirp4netns --help-json` that would print available options in JSON format?

> Will it make sense to have `slirp4netns --help-json` that would print available options in JSON format?

it might be useful in general, but in this case it won't help as the old version of slirp4netns doesn't have it.
I think it is ready for a review :ship:

Tests?

Is there any docs indicated that this is not supported in rootless?

> Tests?

I can add tests once we have an updated slirp4netns in Fedora.  Until then we cannot really test the new feature.

> 
> Is there any docs indicated that this is not supported in rootless?

we support port forwarding now :-)

With this PR and an updated slirp4netns, I can do:

```
$ podman run -p 8080:8080 -d alpine nc -l -p 8080 -e echo hi
696ed647b689c6e64d79560281b6c7c2b391210a18131e83ceb944b49e979c19
$ nc localhost 8080
hi
```
@giuseppe I think he was asking if we need to update docs and/or manpages to say this works now

> @giuseppe I think he was asking if we need to update docs and/or manpages to say this works now

we were not stating it in the man pages, only the runtime error.  So no, I think we don't need to update any documentation.
Good.

LGTM
@mheon @baude @umohnani8 @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL

pushed a new version with more code comments and amended the commit message with the version of slirp4netns needed for port forwarding.

We cannot add tests yet as we need an updated slirp4netns first.
LGTM with one question

/approve
/lgtm

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@rhatdan are we relaying on `--api-socket` instead of command line arguments as in this PR because a pod might have multiple containers?

https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns/pull/58

Should my pull request be accepted, I think this would make this code handling easier.

The static port forwarding case, like here, was exactly the use case which came to my mind for this patch.
But as this needs a new release, I think this is not a high prio.

Sorry if my delay on this upstream pull request was making this more complicated, but the past weeks were quite rough for me.
